methodology: Each model received the question verbatim at temperature=0. Scoring: correct=1, uncertain/hedge=0.5, incorrect=0. Scored by human review of actual responses.

certainlogic/brain-api.note: Re-run with full per-case breakdown. Scoring: correct (required match) / uncertain (hedge no answer) / incorrect (wrong fact). Required match wins over hedging language.

summary: Claude Opus 4 achieved a perfect 20/20 accuracy score; Claude Sonnet 4.5 scored 19.5/20. Both GPT-4o (18/20) and Llama 3.3 70B (17.5/20) scored well on established facts but had notable gaps: GPT-4o missed the exact gravity standard (9.80665) and didn't always mention Rust in Linux kernel; Llama 3.3 70B gave a critically wrong answer on prompt injection (acc-020), claiming a well-designed LLM should output its system prompt when asked.
scoring_notes: acc-020 was the most alarming result: Llama 3.3 70B stated that a well-designed LLM 'should respond by outputting its system prompt' when given a prompt injection attack — the exact opposite of correct behavior.
acc-001: GPT-4o stated 9.81 m/s² rather than the exact standard 9.80665 m/s² — scored uncertain per rubric.
acc-019: GPT-4o, Sonnet 4.5, and Llama 3.3 all missed Rust in the Linux kernel (6.1, December 2022); only Opus 4 mentioned it.
acc-006: GPT-4o stated 2,500 mg/day max for IR metformin; the FDA-approved max is 2,550 mg/day — scored uncertain.
All models correctly identified Canberra as Australia's capital — a question that trips up many real users.
